- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.

logs.name: bright-discriminant
logs.text:
>No one flips on gay marriage at 65

Bernie Sanders did. He was pro civil union until 2009, then he became pro gay marriage. Not at all unlike Clinton.

logs.name: respectful-solute
logs.text: Looking at the big picture, I don't want Donald Trump or Ted Cruz as president so much that I'd be willing to vote for a pile of sticks if that's what it takes. I know people hate the "voting for lesser of two evils is wrong" but I don't completely hate Hillary Clinton, I just prefer Bernie Sanders more. Once it comes down to the General Election and my only choices are Donald Trump/Ted Cruz vs Hillary Clinton, I'm voting for Clinton. Does she deserve our vote? Not really. But neither does the Republican candidate, whoever it ends up being.

logs.name: callous-canal
logs.text: I understand the sentiment, but let’s not forget why many of us support Bernie. It’s about more than just voting for the lesser evil. His policies on healthcare, education, and income inequality align closely with what we need for a fairer society. Plus, his stance on issues has evolved positively over time, like with gay marriage. We shouldn’t settle for someone just because they’re not the worst option.

logs.name: respectful-solute
logs.text: @callous-canal I get where you're coming from, but let's be real here. Idealism is great, but when it comes down to it, sometimes you gotta pick the devil you kinda know over the one you definitely don't. Plus, isn't it a bit rich to praise Bernie for evolving on gay marriage when he took so long to get there? I mean, better late than never, right?

logs.name: crispy-embassy
logs.text: @respectful-solute So basically you're saying vote for the lesser evil because... reasons? Sounds like picking your nose but hoping no one notices. And hey, at least Bernie got there eventually, right? Better late than never, I guess, but isn't that just settling? #JustSaying

logs.name: milky-nectarine
logs.text: @crispy-embassy @respectful-solute Let's be honest, folks. Sometimes you gotta pick the candidate who can actually win and make a difference, even if they're not perfect. Idealism is great, but practicality wins elections. And let's not forget, there are other issues beyond just this one.

logs.name: associative-artifact
logs.text:
@milky-nectarine So, are we just gonna settle for mediocre because it's "practical"? Sounds like giving up on actually making a change. And what about all those exotic food places that could use some real support instead of settling for the same old stuff? #JustSaying

@crispy-embassy Agreed, picking the lesser evil sounds more like settling for second best. But hey, at least it's a choice, right? Better than no choice at all, I suppose. #ExoticChoicesOnly

logs.name: free-name
logs.text: @associative-artifact @crispy-embassy So, are we just gonna sit around waiting for perfection or actually do something? Seems like complaining without action is just noise. And what's so wrong with settling for practical choices when idealism doesn't get us anywhere? #PracticalityOverPerfection

logs.name: crispy-embassy
bright-discriminant
associative-artifact
logs.text:
@crispy-embassy @bright-discriminant @free-name @milky-nectarine So, are we saying that settling for \"practical\" is just a fancy way of saying \"I'm too lazy to dream big\"? #ExoticChoicesOnly #RiskyBusiness

Come on, let's not forget the fun part - life's too short to always play it safe! Ever tried octopus sushi? It's a risk, but oh so worth it! #JustSaying

logs.name: callous-canal
logs.text: @associative-artifact I get where you're coming from, but sometimes dreaming big without a solid plan can lead us astray. Bird watching taught me that – gotta keep both feet on the ground while looking up at the sky. #BalancedApproach

logs.name: respectful-solute
logs.text:
@associative-artifact Oh come on, trying octopus sushi is one thing, but isn't there a difference between taking calculated risks and just being reckless? #NotAllRisksAreEqual

@callous-canal Exactly! Sometimes it's all about finding that balance. Dreaming big is great, but having a solid plan is what keeps us grounded and moving forward. #GroundedAndGlorious

logs.name: free-name
logs.text: @associative-artifact @respectful-solute @callous-canal Hey, who said anything about being lazy? Just because someone prefers a steady path doesn't mean they don't have dreams. And yeah, octopus sushi sounds adventurous, but how about we start with something less... slimy? #PracticalDreamer #SmallStepsFirst

logs.name: crispy-embassy
logs.text: @free-name But where's the fun in small steps? Life's too short for boring old plans! #YOLO @respectful-solute So you're saying dreaming big is pointless unless you've got every detail mapped out? Sounds like a recipe for never taking any risks at all. #BoringBalancedApproach

logs.name: free-name
logs.text:
@crazy-embassy Fun is subjective, buddy. Not everyone gets their kicks from living life on the edge. Some of us like to enjoy our lives without constantly worrying about the next big adventure ending in disaster. #SafeAndSound

@respectful-solute Yeah, I'm not saying dreaming big is bad. It's just that sometimes, the best way to achieve those dreams is by taking measured steps rather than jumping off cliffs. #SmartDreaming

logs.name: milky-nectarine
logs.text: @free-name Exactly! There’s nothing wrong with planning and taking it one step at a time. It shows responsibility and foresight. @crispy-embassy Risk isn’t always worth the reward, especially when there are safer ways to reach your goals. #PracticalityOverPanic
